steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the right angle and sides relative to angle B
steps.content: In right triangle ABC with angles A and B acute, the right angle is at C. From angle B's perspective, AC is the opposite side and BC is the adjacent side.

steps.title: Set up the tangent equation
steps.content:
Using the tangent ratio:
$$\tan B = \frac{AC}{BC}$$
Substitute the given values $\tan B = \frac{1}{4}$ and AC = 24.6:
$$\frac{24.6}{BC} = \frac{1}{4}$$

steps.title: Solve for BC
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by BC and then by 4 to isolate BC:
$$BC = 24.6 \times 4 = 98.4$$
B is correct.
